text: The troposphere extends from Earth's surface to approximately:
explanation: Troposphere: 0–12 km (approx). Contains 75% of atmosphere's mass. Weather occurs here. Temperature decreases with altitude.
name: Troposphere Height

text: The ozone layer is present in the:
explanation: Ozone layer: in stratosphere (mainly 20–35 km). Absorbs harmful UV-B and UV-C radiation. Depleted by CFCs, NOₓ.
name: Ozone Layer Location

options: Reacting with O₂ to form CO₂
Releasing Cl• radicals that catalytically destroy O₃: Cl• + O₃ → ClO• + O₂
explanation: CFC → UV → Cl•. Then: Cl• + O₃ → ClO• + O₂; ClO• + O → Cl• + O₂. Chain reaction: one Cl• destroys ~10⁵ ozone molecules.
name: CFCs and Ozone Depletion

options: Amount of O₂ produced by plants
Amount of O₂ needed by bacteria to decompose organic matter in water
explanation: BOD: amount of O₂ (in ppm) required by bacteria to decompose organic matter in water. High BOD → heavily polluted water (low dissolved O₂).
name: BOD Definition

text: Acid rain is caused primarily by:
options: SO₂ and NOₓ reacting with water to form H₂SO₄ and HNO₃
explanation: Acid rain: SO₂ + H₂O + ½O₂ → H₂SO₄; NOₓ + H₂O → HNO₃. pH < 5.6. Damages buildings (marble), kills fish, harms forests.
name: Acid Rain Causes

text: The greenhouse effect is caused by:
options: CO₂, CH₄, N₂O, H₂O vapour, CFCs (greenhouse gases)
explanation: Greenhouse gases: CO₂, CH₄, N₂O, H₂O, CFCs. Absorb IR radiation emitted by Earth → trap heat → global warming.
name: Greenhouse Gases

options: NOₓ + unburnt hydrocarbons + UV → O₃, PAN, aldehydes
explanation: Photochemical smog: NOₓ + HC (UV light) → O₃ + PAN (peroxyacetyl nitrate) + formaldehyde. Brown haze, causes eye/lung irritation.
name: Photochemical Smog

text: PAN (peroxyacetyl nitrate) is a component of:
explanation: PAN (CH₃CO–O–O–NO₂): formed in photochemical smog. Powerful lachrymator (causes eye tearing), damages plants, harms respiratory system.
name: PAN in Smog

text: The permissible limit of fluoride in drinking water is:
explanation: WHO/BIS: fluoride 1–1.5 ppm in drinking water. < 0.5 ppm → dental caries; > 1.5 ppm → fluorosis (dental mottling); > 10 ppm → skeletal fluorosis.
name: Fluoride in Drinking Water

text: Eutrophication of water bodies is caused by:
options: Excess nutrients (nitrates/phosphates) leading to algal bloom and O₂ depletion
explanation: Eutrophication: excess N and P (from fertilisers, sewage) → algal bloom → decomposition consumes O₂ → fish kills (hypoxia).
name: Eutrophication

explanation: Minamata disease (Japan, 1950s): caused by methylmercury (CH₃Hg⁺) contamination of fish/shellfish. Neurological damage, paralysis. Itai-itai disease: Cd.
name: Minamata Disease

text: The major source of CO in the atmosphere is:
options: Incomplete combustion of fossil fuels in vehicles and industries
explanation: CO: primarily from incomplete combustion of fossil fuels (vehicle exhaust). CO binds haemoglobin 200× more strongly than O₂ → asphyxiation.
name: Source of CO Pollution

text: Classical (London) smog is primarily composed of:
options: SO₂ + particulates (soot/fog) in cold, damp conditions
explanation: Classical smog (London type): SO₂ + particulates/smoke + fog. Formed in cool, damp, stagnant air. Opposite: photochemical smog (warm, sunny).
name: Classical Smog

text: Biochemical oxygen demand (BOD) of clean drinking water is:
explanation: Clean water: BOD < 1 ppm. Moderately polluted: ~5 ppm. Heavily polluted: > 17 ppm (needs treatment before use).
name: BOD of Clean Water

text: The Montreal Protocol (1987) was established to:
options: Phase out ozone-depleting substances (CFCs, HCFCs, halons)
explanation: Montreal Protocol: international treaty to phase out CFCs, HCFCs, halons (ozone-depleting substances). Successful — ozone layer is slowly recovering.
name: Montreal Protocol
